mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and stock prices. Sustained increases in this series signal that the economy is expanding, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If over any two-year window, months with RSAFS above its trailing 12-month average are followed by negative median 20-day SPX returns, or rank IC < 0.03 across all months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4%, it signals active monetary tightening to restrain growth. Higher rates compress equity valuations via increased discount rates and reduced corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, or the average negative return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirm that both supply and labor demand are expanding, a robust signal of economic acceleration that should lift equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: If the combined signal (both series above their 3-month moving averages) is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any rolling year.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment (UNRATE) boosts stocks most strongly when inflation (CPIAUCSL) is low and unemployment is still elevated (above 5%). In such a high-slack, low-inflation recovery regime, labour market improvement directly raises consumption without triggering wage-push inflation, yielding positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If during periods when UNRATE > 5% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year change < 2%, a 0.3pp drop in UNRATE over 3 months does not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strengthening labour market, which supports consumer income and spending, driving corporate profits and stock prices upward. The signal is always active as a positive indicator.
failure_condition: If months with PAYEMS above its 12-month moving average are followed by negative median 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts exceed 2000 thousand units, it signals a robust housing market recovery. Residential investment boosts overall economic activity, and the sustained high level of construction is a leading indicator for positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If HOUST > 2000 for two consecutive months and the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and inflation are rising simultaneously, it signals that the central bank is actively tightening to combat above-target inflation. This dual increase compresses equity valuations via higher discount rates and reduced future cash flow expectations, leading to negative short-term stocks returns.
failure_condition: If months where FEDFUNDS rises from previous month and CPIAUCSL accelerates (MoM change > 0.2) result in positive median 20-day SPX returns more than 45% of the time over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) is a broad measure of output from manufacturing, mining, and utilities. An increasing trend indicates solid economic activity and rising corporate earnings. The signal is always on and positively correlated with equity returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If weeks with INDPRO above its 3-month moving average are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances across any 2-year period.
